finding: The durable terminal journal can classify a post-rename directory-sync failure as recorded-indeterminate-reconciled, but the peer execution runtime previously accepted only recorded and already-recorded. A real filesystem-backed runtime adapter must translate that reconciled durable outcome into an append-safe recorded receipt while preserving the distinct durability state for evidence consumers.
source_status: primary operating-system interface specifications plus committed repository behavior
design_implication: Construct peer execution runtimes through a filesystem adapter that binds the existing proof verifier, compare-and-set journal, and atomic filesystem store. Preserve recorded-indeterminate-reconciled as durability_state while returning recorded to the caller only after reread reconciliation proves the exact terminal is durable.

checks: adapter imports and wires the existing runtime, verifier, durable journal, and filesystem store
normal writes retain recorded/already-recorded idempotency semantics
injected directory-sync failure must preserve recorded-indeterminate-reconciled in durability_state
terminal event append occurs once after exact reread reconciliation
verification.runtime_limit: The automation container could not resolve github.com, so the committed Node test was not executed in a fresh checkout during this run.

evidence_strength: implementation committed; deterministic test committed; runtime execution pending
uncertainty: Cross-process crash injection and a CI execution remain unverified.
falsification_route: Run node --test tools/frontier-research/filesystem-peer-execution-runtime.test.mjs in CI. Fail the slice if a reconciled terminal is rejected, if durability_state is lost, if the event is appended more than once, or if a conflicting terminal is accepted.
privacy_boundary: Public repository code and generic runtime metadata only; no private chat, health, credentials, or identifying personal material.
